name: SmartUI Precision Agent Doctrine
purpose: Make UI agents diagnose precisely before patching and keep mobile UI consistent across pages.
coreRules: Never guess UI causes from screenshots only when code/DOM can be diagnosed.
Before patching UI, compare static HTML/CSS, rendered DOM, computed CSS, and screenshot evidence.
Use a known-good page/component as baseline when a page looks inconsistent.

When a component differs, identify exact deltas: markup, class names, active state, width, height, padding, gap, fontSize, fontWeight, lineHeight, border, background, icon wrapper, and click/focus states.
Do not stack random CSS overrides. First remove stale/duplicate patch blocks, then apply one clean final block.
Do not use broad global replacements unless diagnosis proves it is safe.
When bottom nav differs, compare all pages and use one master nav structure. Markup consistency beats CSS patch stacking.
Always test active, hover, focus, and click state because UI can change after tap/click.
When a UI bug appears after navigation, verify before/after rect stability.
For SmartWork mobile pages, bottom nav should remain stable: same width, height, item width, icon size, label size, gap, and active bubble across Home, Request, Progress, Riwayat, Profil.
Avoid opening many terminal windows. Do not use powershell -NoExit for diagnosis. Prefer current terminal or background Job.
Every UI patch must create backup, patch, run SmartUI check, save report JSON, save screenshot, and print concise summary.
If a patch makes UI worse, rollback from backup first, then diagnose; do not continue layering patches.

staticSource: Find matching HTML block in all relevant pages.
Compare class names and element wrappers.
Find old patch markers and duplicated CSS blocks.
renderedDOM: Capture boundingClientRect for container, items, icon wrapper, svg, label.
Capture computed styles for display, grid/flex, width, height, padding, margin, gap, font, line-height, background, transform.
interactionState: Check active/focus/hover selectors do not change width or height.
visualProof: Only patch when exact diff is known.

kpiCardStandard.rule: Do not split KPI text with JavaScript when HTML already has statIcon/statNum/statLabel.
kpiCardStandard.layout: Use CSS grid: icon left spanning two rows, number top right, label bottom right.
kpiCardStandard.antiPattern: Do not run DOM scripts that delete or rewrite stat cards unless static markup is broken.
